metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3030
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.2: "PKA lengthens the spontaneous NMDAR current decay time in opposition to CaN"
- "Figure 2B (top two traces) shows the average NMDAR current from a single neuron in which the PKA-inhibitory peptide was introduced into the recording pipette solution (Wang et al., 1994b). After break-in, the cell was dialyzed for 20 min, and the decay times of spontaneous NMDAR currents were recorded. NMDAR current kinetics was fast, which is typical of other P11 neurons. However, when FK506 was added to the perfusion media, the PKA-inhibitory peptide prevented the lengthening of the NMDAR current decay time that normally occurred with CaN antagonists."

Note: the authors show that PRKACA mediates the NMDA current decay time. Because the data do not show whether the intrinsic channel properties or NTR trafficking are affected I used the less specific term 'postsynaptic modulation of chemical synaptic transmission (GO:0099170)'.. Experimental description: - " NR2A knock-out mice were produced in the laboratory of M. Mishina ..."
- The authors use an PKA inhibitory peptide from Upstate (20-120 | PKA Inhibitor Peptide) that claims to inhibit PRKACA specifically. I had no way to verify this claim, but decided to accept it.
